description: Contemporary research topics in the geographic information sciences. Topic varies annually, but are selected from one of the following: spatial decision support; visualization; business demographics; environmental modeling; or spatial analysis.
preAndCorequisites:
• Complete 1 of:
    • GEOG328 - GIS Analysis (1.5)
    • GEOG329 - GIS Applications and Tools (1.5)
recommendations: • GEOG 328 and GEOG 329 recommended prior to GEOG 428.
supplementalNotes:
• Credit will be granted for only one of GEOG 428, GEOG 423.
  • International students interested in taking GEOG 428 should contact the International Centre for Students (ICS) to discuss potential work authorization requirements for the experiential learning component of this course.
title: Advanced Topics in Geographic Information Sciences
